36.4: So Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) called Baruch (blessed) son of Neriah (lamp of YHWH), and at the dictation of Jeremiah, Baruch wrote on a scroll all the words that the LORD {YHWH} had spoken to Jeremiah.
36.5: Then Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) commanded Baruch (blessed), “I am restricted; I cannot enter the house of the LORD {YHWH};
36.6: so you are to go to the house of the LORD {YHWH} on a day of fasting, and in the hearing of the people you are to read the words of the LORD from the scroll you have written at my dictation. Read them in the hearing of all the people of Judah (praised) who are coming from their cities.
36.7: Perhaps they will bring their petition before the LORD {YHWH}, and each one will turn from his wicked way; for great are the anger and fury that the LORD has pronounced against this people.”
36.8: So Baruch (blessed) son of Neriah (lamp of YHWH) did everything that Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet had commanded him. In the house of the LORD {YHWH} he read the words of the LORD from the scroll.
36.9: Now in the ninth month of the fifth year of Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) son of Josiah (whom YHWH heals) king of Judah (praised), a fast before the LORD {YHWH} was proclaimed to all the people of Jerusalem (city of peace) and all who had come there from the cities of Judah.
36.10: {YHWH} From the chamber of Gemariah (YHWH has accomplished) son of Shaphan the scribe, which was in the upper courtyard at the opening of the New Gate of the house of the LORD, Baruch (blessed) read from the scroll the words of Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) in the hearing of all the people.
36.11: When Micaiah (who is like God) son of Gemariah (YHWH has accomplished), the son of Shaphan, heard all the words of the LORD {YHWH} from the scroll,
36.12: he went down to the scribe’s chamber in the king’s palace, where all the officials were sitting: Elishama (my God has heard) the scribe, Delaiah (YHWH has drawn) son of Shemaiah (heard by YHWH), Elnathan (God has given) son of Achbor (mouse), Gemariah (YHWH has accomplished) son of Shaphan, Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) son of Hananiah (God has favored), and all the other officials.
36.13: And Micaiah (who is like God) reported to them all the words he had heard Baruch (blessed) read from the scroll in the hearing of the people.
36.14: Then all the officials sent word to Baruch (blessed) through Jehudi (Jew) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH), the son of Shelemiah (repaid by YHWH), the son of Cushi (their blackness), saying, “Bring the scroll that you read in the hearing of the people, and come here. So Baruch son of Neriah (lamp of YHWH) took the scroll and went to them.
36.15: “Please sit down, they said, “and read it in our hearing. So Baruch (blessed) read it in their hearing.
36.16: When they had heard all these words, they turned to one another in fear and said to Baruch (blessed), “Surely we must report all these words to the king.”
36.17: “Tell us now,” they asked Baruch (blessed), “how did you write all these words? Was it at Jeremiah’s dictation?”
36.18: “It was at his dictation, Baruch (blessed) replied. “He recited all these words to me and I wrote them in ink on the scroll.”
36.19: Then the officials said to Baruch (blessed), “You and Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) must hide yourselves and tell no one where you? are.”
36.20: So the officials went to the king in the courtyard. And having stored the scroll in the chamber of Elishama (my God has heard) the scribe, they reported everything to the king.
36.21: Then the king sent Jehudi (Jew) to get the scroll, and he took it from the chamber of Elishama (my God has heard) the scribe. And Jehudi read it in the hearing of the king and all the officials who were standing beside him.
36.22: Since it was the ninth month, the king was sitting in his winter quarters with a fire burning before him.
36.23: And as soon as Jehudi (Jew) had read three or four columns, Jehoiakim would cut them off with a scribe’s knife and throw them into the firepot, until the entire scroll had been consumed by the fire.
36.24: Yet in hearing all these words, the king and his servants did not become frightened or tear their garments.
36.25: Even though Elnathan (God has given), Delaiah (YHWH has drawn), and Gemariah (YHWH has accomplished) urged the king not to burn the scroll, he would not listen to them.
36.26: Instead, the king commanded Jerahmeel (may God have pity), a son of the king, as well as Seraiah (YHWH is ruler) son of Azriel (my help is God) and Shelemiah (repaid by YHWH) son of Abdeel (servant of God), to seize Baruch (blessed) the scribe and Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet. But the LORD {YHWH} had hidden them.
36.27: After the king had burned the scroll containing the words that Baruch (blessed) had written at Jeremiah’s (whom YHWH has appointed) dictation, the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Jeremiah:
36.28: “Take another scroll and rewrite on it the very words that were on the original scroll, which Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) king of Judah (praised) has burned.
36.29: You are to proclaim concerning Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) king of Judah (praised) that this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: You have burned the scroll and said, ‘Why have you written on it that the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) would surely come and destroy this land and deprive it of man and beast?’
36.30: Therefore this is what the LORD {YHWH} says about Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) king of Judah (praised): He will have no one to sit on David’s (beloved) throne, and his body will be thrown out and exposed to heat by day and frost by night.
36.31: I will punish him and his descendants and servants for their iniquity. I will bring on them, on the residents of Jerusalem (city of peace), and on the men of Judah (praised), all the calamity about which I warned them but they did not listen.”
36.32: Then Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) took another scroll and gave it to the scribe Baruch (blessed) son of Neriah (lamp of YHWH), and at Jeremiah’s dictation he wrote on it all the words of the scroll that Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) king of Judah (praised) had burned in the fire. And many similar words were added to them.

37.1: Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) made Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) son of Josiah (whom YHWH heals) the king of Judah (praised), and he reigned in place of Coniah (Jehoiachin) son of Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up).
37.2: But he and his officers and the people of the land refused to obey the words that the LORD {YHWH} had spoken through Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet.
37.3: Yet King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) sent Jehucal (Jucal) son of Shelemiah (repaid by YHWH) and Zephaniah (YHWH has treasured) the priest, the son of Maaseiah (work of YHWH), to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet with the message, “Please pray to the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu} for us!”
37.4: Now Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) was free to come and go among the people, for they had not yet put him in prison.
37.5: Pharaoh’s (great house) army had left Egypt (land of bondage), and when the Chaldeans (the Babylonians) who were besieging Jerusalem (city of peace) heard the report, they withdrew from Jerusalem.
37.6: Then the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet:
37.7: “This is what the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says that you? are to tell the king of Judah (praised), who sent you? to Me: Behold, Pharaoh’s (great house) army, which has marched out to help you?, will go back to its own land of Egypt (land of bondage).
37.8: Then the Chaldeans (clod-breakers) will return and fight against this city. They will capture it and burn it down.
37.9: This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: Do not deceive yourselves by saying, ‘The Chaldeans (clod-breakers) will go away for good, for they will not!
37.10: Indeed, if you? were to strike down the entire army of the Chaldeans (clod-breakers) that is fighting against you?, and only wounded men remained in their tents, they would still get up and burn this city down.”
37.11: When the Chaldean (clod-breakers) army withdrew from Jerusalem (city of peace) for fear of Pharaoh’s (great house) army,
37.12: Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) started to leave Jerusalem (city of peace) to go to the land of Benjamin (son of the right hand) to claim his portion there \to divide from there/ among the people.
37.13: But when he reached the Gate of Benjamin (son of the right hand), the captain of the guard, whose name was Irijah (YHWH sees me) son of Shelemiah (repaid by YHWH), the son of Hananiah (God has favored), seized him (whom YHWH has appointed) and said, “You are deserting to the Chaldeans (clod-breakers)!”
37.14: “That is a lie, Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) replied. “I am not deserting to the Chaldeans (clod-breakers)! But Irijah (YHWH sees me) would not listen to him; instead, he arrested Jeremiah and took him to the officials.
37.15: The officials were angry with Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), and they beat him and placed him in jail in the house of Jonathan (YHWH has given) the scribe, for it had been made into a prison.
37.16: So Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) went into a cell in the dungeon and remained there a long time.
37.17: Later, King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) sent for Jeremiah and received him in his palace, where he asked him privately, “Is there a word from the LORD {YHWH}? “There is, Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) replied. “You will be delivered into the hand of the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing).”
37.18: Then Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) asked King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous), “How have I sinned against you or your servants or these people, that you? have put me in prison?
37.19: Where are your? prophets who prophesied to you?, claiming, ‘The king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) will not come against you? or this land’
37.20: But now please listen, O my lord the king. May my petition come before you. Do not send me back to the house of Jonathan (YHWH has given) the scribe, or I will die there.”
37.21: So King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) gave orders for Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) to be placed in the courtyard of the guard and given a loaf of bread daily from the street of the bakers, until all the bread in the city was gone. So Jeremiah remained in the courtyard of the guard.

38.1: Now Shephatiah (YHWH has judged) son of Mattan (a gift), Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Pashhur (freedom), Jucal (Jehucal) son of Shelemiah (repaid by YHWH), and Pashhur son of Malchijah (my king is YHWH) heard that Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) had been telling all the people:
38.2: “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: Whoever stays in this city will die by sword and famine and plague, but whoever surrenders to the Chaldeans (the Babylonians) will live; he will retain his life like a spoil of war, and he will live.
38.3: This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: This city will surely be delivered into the hands of the army of the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), and he will capture it.
38.4: Then the officials said to the king, “This man ought to die, for he is discouraging the warriors who remain in this city, as well as all the people, by speaking such words to them; this man is not seeking the well-being of these people, but their ruin.”
38.5: “Here he is,” replied King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous). “He is in your? hands, since the king can do nothing to stop you?.”
38.6: So they took Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) and dropped him into the cistern of Malchiah (my king is YHWH), the king’s son, which was in the courtyard of the guard. They lowered Jeremiah with ropes into the cistern, which had no water but only mud, and Jeremiah sank down into the mud.
38.7: Now Ebed-melech (servant of the king) the Cushite, a court official (eunuch) in the royal palace, heard that Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) had been put into the cistern. While the king was sitting at the Gate of Benjamin (son of the right hand),
38.8: Ebed-melech (servant of the king) went out from the king’s palace and said to the king,
38.9: “My lord the king, these men have acted wickedly in all that they have done to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet. They have dropped him into the cistern, where he will starve to death, for there is no more bread in the city.”
38.10: So the king commanded Ebed-melech (servant of the king) the Cushite, “Take thirty men from here with you and pull Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet out of the cistern before he dies.”
38.11: Then Ebed-melech (servant of the king) took the men with him and went to the king’s palace, to a place below the storehouse. From there he took old rags and worn-out clothes and lowered them with ropes to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) in the cistern.
38.12: Ebed-melech (servant of the king) the Cushite cried out to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), “Put these worn-out rags and clothes under your arms to pad the ropes. Jeremiah did so,
38.13: and they pulled him (whom YHWH has appointed) up with the ropes and lifted him out of the cistern. And Jeremiah remained in the courtyard of the guard.
38.14: Then King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) sent for Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet and received him at the third entrance to the house of the LORD {YHWH}. “I am going to ask you something, said the king to Jeremiah. “Do not hide anything from me.”
38.15: “If I tell you, Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) replied (YHWH is righteous), “you will surely put me to death. And even if I give you advice, you will not listen to me.”
38.16: But King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) swore secretly to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), “As surely as the LORD {YHWH} lives, who has given us this life, I will not kill you, nor will I deliver you into the hands of these men who are seeking your life.”
38.17: Then Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) said to Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous), “This is what the LORD God {YHWH Elohe} of Hosts, the God of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: ‘If you indeed surrender to the officers of the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), then you will live, this city will not be burned down, and you and your household will survive.
38.18: But if you do not surrender to the officers of the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), then this city will be delivered into the hands of the Chaldeans (clod-breakers). They will burn it down, and you yourself will not escape their grasp.
38.19: But King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) said to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), “I am afraid of the Jews who have deserted to the Chaldeans (clod-breakers), for the Chaldeans may deliver me into their hands to abuse me.”
38.20: “They will not hand you over, Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) replied. “Obey the voice of the LORD {YHWH} in what I am telling you, that it may go well with you and you may live.
38.21: But if you refuse to surrender, this is the word that the LORD {YHWH} has shown me:
38.22: All the women who remain in the palace of the king of Judah (praised) will be brought out to the officials of the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), and those women will say: ‘They misled you and overcame you—those trusted friends of yours. Your feet sank into the mire, and they deserted you.
38.23: All your wives and children will be brought out to the Chaldeans (clod-breakers). And you yourself will not escape their grasp, for you will be seized by the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), and this city will be burned down.
38.24: Then Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) warned Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), “Do not let anyone know about this conversation, or you will die.
38.25: If the officials hear that I have spoken with you, and they come and demand of you, ‘Tell us what you said to the king and what he said to you; do not hide it from us, or we will kill you,
38.26: then tell them, ‘I was presenting to the king my petition that he not return me to the house of Jonathan (YHWH has given) to die there.
38.27: When all the officials came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) and questioned him, he relayed to them the exact words the king had commanded him to say. So they said no more to him, for no one had overheard the conversation.
38.28: And Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) remained in the courtyard of the guard until the day Jerusalem (city of peace) was captured.

39.1: In the ninth year of Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) king of Judah (praised), in the tenth month, Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) marched against Jerusalem (city of peace) with his entire army and laid siege to the city.
39.2: And on the ninth day of the fourth month of Zedekiah’s (YHWH is righteous) eleventh year, the city was breached.
39.3: Then all the officials of the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) entered and sat in the Middle Gate: Nergal-sharezer of Samgar, Nebo-sarsekim the Rabsaris (chief eunuch), Nergal-sharezer the Rabmag (chief soothsayer), and all the rest of the officials of the king of Babylon.
39.4: When Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) king of Judah (praised) and all the soldiers saw them, they fled. They left the city at night by way of the king’s garden, through the gate between the two walls, and they went out along the route to the Arabah (the Jordan Valley).
39.5: But the army of the Chaldeans (the Babylonians) pursued them and overtook Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) in the plains of Jericho (city of palms). They seized him and brought him up to Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) at Riblah (fertility) in the land of Hamath (fortress), where he pronounced judgment on him.
39.6: There at Riblah (fertility) the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) before his eyes, and he also killed all the nobles of Judah (praised).
39.7: Then he put out Zedekiah’s (YHWH is righteous) eyes and bound him with bronze chains to take him to Babylon (confusion by mixing).
39.8: The Chaldeans (clod-breakers) set fire to the palace of the king and to the houses of the people, and they broke down the walls of Jerusalem (city of peace).
39.9: Then Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard carried away to Babylon (confusion by mixing) the remnant of the people who had remained in the city, along with the deserters who had defected to him.
39.10: But Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) left behind in the land of Judah (praised) some of the poor people who had no property, and at that time he gave them vineyards and fields.
39.11: Now Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) had given orders about Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) through Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard, saying,
39.12: “Take him, look after him, and do not let any harm come to him; do for him whatever he says.”
39.13: So Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard, Nebushazban (Nebo-sarsekim) the Rabsaris, Nergal-sharezer (prince of fire) the Rabmag, and all the captains of the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing)
39.14: had Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) brought from the courtyard of the guard, and they turned him over to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen), the son of Shaphan, to take him home. So Jeremiah remained among his own people.
39.15: And while Jeremiah had been confined in the courtyard of the guard, the word of the LORD {YHWH} had come to him (whom YHWH has appointed):
39.16: “Go and tell Ebed-melech (servant of the king) the Cushite that this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: ‘I am about to fulfill My words against this city for harm and not for good, and on that day they will be fulfilled before your eyes.
39.17: But I will deliver you on that day, declares the LORD {YHWH}, and you will not be delivered into the hands of the men whom you fear.
39.18: For I will surely rescue you so that you do not fall by the sword. Because you have trusted in Me, you will escape with your life like a spoil of war, declares the LORD {YHWH}.

40.1: This is the word that came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) from the LORD {YHWH} after Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard had released him at Ramah (hill), having found him bound in chains among all the captives of Jerusalem (city of peace) and Judah (praised) who were being exiled to Babylon (confusion by mixing).
40.2: The captain of the guard found Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) and said to him, “The LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} decreed this disaster on this place,
40.3: and now the LORD {YHWH} has fulfilled it; He has done just as He said. Because you? people have sinned against the LORD and have not obeyed His voice, this thing has happened to you?.
40.4: But now, behold, I am freeing you today from the chains that were on your wrists. If it pleases you to come with me to Babylon (confusion by mixing), then come, and I will take care of you. But if it seems wrong to you to come with me to Babylon, go no farther. Look, the whole land is before you. Wherever it seems good and right to you, go there.”
40.5: But before Jeremiah turned to go, Nebuzaradan added, “Return to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen), the son of Shaphan, whom the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) has appointed over the cities of Judah (praised), and stay with him among the people, or go anywhere else that seems right. Then the captain of the guard gave him a ration and a gift and released him.
40.6: So Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) went to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen) at Mizpah (watchtower) and stayed with him among the people who were left in the land.
40.7: When all the commanders and men of the armies in the field heard that the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) had appointed Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen) over the land and that he had put him in charge of the men, women, and children who were the poorest of the land and had not been exiled to Babylon,
40.8: they came to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) at Mizpah (watchtower)—Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH), Johanan (YHWH has graced) and Jonathan (YHWH has given) the sons of Kareah (bald), Seraiah (YHWH is ruler) son of Tanhumeth (consolation), the sons of Ephai (gloomy) the Netophathite, and Jezaniah (Jaazaniah) son of the Maacathite (she has pressed)—they and their men.
40.9: Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen), the son of Shaphan, swore an oath to them and their men, assuring them, “Do not be afraid to serve the Chaldeans (the Babylonians). Live in the land and serve the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), and it will go well with you?.
40.10: As for me, I will stay in Mizpah (watchtower) to represent you before the Chaldeans (clod-breakers) who come to us. As for you?, gather wine grapes, summer fruit, and oil, place them in your? storage jars, and live in the cities you? have taken.”
40.11: When all the Jews in Moab (of his father), Ammon, (tribal) Edom (red), and all the other lands heard that the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) had left a remnant in Judah (praised) and had appointed Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen), the son of Shaphan, over them,
40.12: they all returned from all the places to which they had been banished and came to the land of Judah (praised), to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) at Mizpah (watchtower). And they gathered an abundance of wine grapes and summer fruit.
40.13: Meanwhile, Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald) and all the commanders of the armies in the field came to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) at Mizpah (watchtower)
40.14: and said to him, “Are you aware that Baalis (lord of the banner) king of the Ammonites (tribal) has sent Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) to take your life? But Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen) did not believe them.
40.15: Then Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald) spoke privately to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) at Mizpah (watchtower). “Let me go and kill Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH), he said. “No one will know it. Why should he take your life and scatter all the people of Judah (praised) who have gathered to you, so that the remnant of Judah would perish?”
40.16: But Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen) said to Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald), “Do not do such a thing! What you are saying about Ishmael (God hears) is a lie.”

41.1: In the seventh month, Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH), the son of Elishama (my God has heard), who was a member of the royal family and one of the king’s chief officers, came with ten men to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen) at Mizpah (watchtower), and they ate a meal together there.
41.2: Then Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) and the ten men who were with him got up and struck down Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen), the son of Shaphan, with the sword, killing the one whom the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) had appointed to govern the land.
41.3: Ishmael (God hears) also killed all the Jews who were with Gedaliah (YHWH is great) at Mizpah (watchtower), as well as the Chaldean (Babylonian) soldiers who were there.
41.4: On the second day after the murder of Gedaliah (YHWH is great), when no one yet knew about it,
41.5: eighty men who had shaved off their beards, torn their garments, and cut themselves came from Shechem (shoulder), Shiloh (place of rest), and Samaria (watch mountain), carrying grain offerings and frankincense for the house of the LORD {YHWH}.
41.6: And Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) went out from Mizpah (watchtower) to meet them, weeping as he went. When Ishmael encountered the men, he said, “Come to Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen).”
41.7: And when they came into the city, Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) and the men with him slaughtered them and threw them into a cistern.
41.8: But ten of the men among them said to Ishmael (God hears), “Do not kill us, for we have hidden treasure in the field—wheat, barley, oil, and honey! So he refrained from killing them with the others.
41.9: Now the cistern into which Ishmael (God hears) had thrown all the bodies of the men he had struck down along with Gedaliah (YHWH is great) was a large one that King Asa (healer) had made for fear of Baasha (wicked) king of Israel (he wrestles with God). Ishmael son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) filled it with the slain.
41.10: Then Ishmael (God hears) took captive all the remnant of the people of Mizpah (watchtower)—the daughters of the king along with all the others who remained in Mizpah—over whom Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard had appointed Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen). Ishmael son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) took them captive and set off to cross over to the Ammonites (tribal).
41.11: When Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald) and all the commanders of the armies with him heard of all the crimes that Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) had committed,
41.12: they took all their men and went to fight Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH). And they found him near the great pool in Gibeon (hill city).
41.13: When all the people with Ishmael (God hears) saw Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald) and all the commanders of the army with him, they rejoiced,
41.14: and all the people whom Ishmael (God hears) had taken captive at Mizpah (watchtower) turned and went over to Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald).
41.15: But Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) and eight of his men escaped from Johanan (YHWH has graced) and went to the Ammonites (tribal).
41.16: Then Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald) and all the commanders of the armies with him took the whole remnant of the people from Mizpah (watchtower) whom he had recovered from Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) after Ishmael had killed Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen): the soldiers, women, children, and court officials (eunuchs) he had brought back from Gibeon (hill city).
41.17: And they went and stayed in Geruth Chimham, near Bethlehem (house of bread), in order to proceed into Egypt (land of bondage)
41.18: to escape the Chaldeans (the Babylonians). For they were afraid of the Chaldeans because Ishmael (God hears) son of Nethaniah (given of YHWH) had struck down Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen), whom the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) had appointed over the land.

42.1: Then all the commanders of the forces, along with Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald), Jezaniah [Azariah] son of Hoshaiah (YHWH as saved), and all the people from the least to the greatest, approached
42.2: Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet and said, “May our petition come before you; pray to the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} on behalf of this entire remnant. For few of us remain of the many, as you can see with your own eyes.
42.3: Pray that the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} will tell us the way we should walk and the thing we should do.”
42.4: “I have heard you, replied Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet. “I will surely pray to the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} as you? request, and I will tell you? everything that the LORD {YHWH} answers. I will not withhold a word from you?.”
42.5: Then they said to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), “May the LORD {YHWH} be a true and faithful witness against us if we do not act upon every word that the LORD your God {YHWH Eloheka} sends you? to tell us.
42.6: Whether it is pleasant or unpleasant, we will obey the voice of the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu} to whom we are sending you, so that it may go well with us when we obey the voice of the LORD our God!”
42.7: After ten days the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed),
42.8: and he summoned Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald), all the commanders of the forces who were with him, and all the people from the least to the greatest.
42.9: Jeremiah told them, “Thus says the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), to whom you? sent me to present your? petition:
42.10: ‘If you? will indeed stay in this land, then I will build you? up and not tear you down; I will plant you? and not uproot you, for I will relent of the disaster I have brought upon you?.
42.11: Do not be afraid of the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), whom you? now fear; do not be afraid of him, declares the LORD {YHWH}, for I am with you? to save you? and deliver you? from him.
42.12: And I will show you? compassion, and he will have compassion on you? and restore you? to your? own land.
42.13: But if you? say, ‘We will not stay in this land,’ and you thus disobey the voice of the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem},
42.14: and if you say, ‘No, but we will go to the land of Egypt (land of bondage) and live there, where we will not see war or hear the sound of the ram’s horn or hunger for bread,’
42.15: then hear the word of the LORD {YHWH}, O remnant of Judah (praised)! This is what the LORD of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: ‘If you? are determined to go to Egypt (land of bondage) and reside there,
42.16: then the sword you? fear will overtake you? there (land of bondage), and the famine you? dread will follow on your? heels into Egypt, and you? will die there.
42.17: So all who resolve to go to Egypt (land of bondage) to reside there will die by sword and famine and plague. Not one of them will survive or escape the disaster I will bring upon them.
42.18: For this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: ‘Just as My anger and wrath were poured out on the residents of Jerusalem (city of peace), so will My wrath be poured out on you? if you? go to Egypt (land of bondage). You? will become an object of cursing and horror, of vilification and disgrace, and you? will never see this place again.’
42.19: The LORD {YHWH} has told you?, O remnant of Judah (praised), ‘Do not go to Egypt (land of bondage). Know for sure that I have warned you? today!
42.20: For you? have deceived yourselves by sending me to the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem}, saying, ‘Pray to the LORD our God on our behalf, and as for all that the LORD our God says, tell it to us and we will do it.’
42.21: For I have told you? today, but you? have not obeyed the voice of the LORD your? God {YHWH Eloheikem} in all He has sent me to tell you?.
42.22: Now therefore, know for sure that by sword and famine and plague you? will die in the place where you? desire to go to reside.”

43.1: When Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) had finished telling all the people all the words of the LORD their God {YHWH Elohehem}—everything that the LORD had sent him to say—
43.2: Azariah (YHWH has helped) son of Hoshaiah (YHWH as saved), Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald), and all the arrogant men said to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed), “You are lying! The LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu} has not sent you to say, ‘You must not go to Egypt (land of bondage) to reside there.’
43.3: Rather, Baruch (blessed) son of Neriah (lamp of YHWH) is inciting you against us to deliver us into the hands of the Chaldeans (the Babylonians), so that they may put us to death or exile us to Babylon (confusion by mixing)!”
43.4: So Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald) and all the commanders of the forces disobeyed the command of the LORD {YHWH} to stay in the land of Judah (praised).
43.5: Instead, Johanan (YHWH has graced) son of Kareah (bald) and all the commanders of the forces took the whole remnant of Judah (praised), those who had returned to the land of Judah from all the nations to which they had been scattered,
43.6: the men, the women, the children, the king’s daughters, and everyone whom Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard had allowed to remain with Gedaliah (YHWH is great) son of Ahikam (my brother has arisen), the son of Shaphan, as well as Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet and Baruch (blessed) son of Neriah (lamp of YHWH).
43.7: So they entered the land of Egypt (land of bondage) because they did not obey the voice of the LORD {YHWH}, and they went as far as Tahpanhes (You will fill hands with pity).
43.8: Then the word of the LORD {YHWH} came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) at Tahpanhes (You will fill hands with pity):
43.9: “In the sight of the Jews, pick up some large stones and bury them in the clay of the brick pavement at the entrance to Pharaoh’s (great house) palace at Tahpanhes (You will fill hands with pity).
43.10: Then tell them that this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: ‘I will send for My servant Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), and I will set his throne over these stones that I have embedded, and he will spread his royal pavilion over them.
43.11: He will come and strike down the land of Egypt (land of bondage), bringing death to those destined for death, captivity to those destined for captivity, and the sword to those destined for the sword.
43.12: I will kindle a fire in the temples of the gods of Egypt (land of bondage), and Nebuchadnezzar will burn those temples and take their gods as captives \and he will burn them and take them captive/. So he will wrap himself with the land of Egypt as a shepherd wraps himself in his garment, and he will depart from there unscathed.
43.13: He will demolish the sacred pillars of the temple of the sun (house of the sun) in the land of Egypt [He will demolish the pillars of Heliopolis in On], and he will burn down the temples of the gods of Egypt (land of bondage).’”

44.1: This is the word that came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) concerning all the Jews living in the land of Egypt (Lower Egypt) in Migdol (tower), Tahpanhes (You will fill hands with pity), and Memphis [Noph] and in the land of Pathros (region of the south) (Upper Egypt)
44.2: “This is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: You? have seen all the disaster that I brought against Jerusalem (city of peace) and all the cities of Judah (praised); and behold, they lie today in ruins and desolation
44.3: because of the evil they have done. They provoked Me to anger by continuing to burn incense and to serve other gods that neither they nor you? nor your? fathers ever knew.
44.4: Yet I sent you? all My servants the prophets again and again \the prophets, rising up early and sending them,/, saying: ‘Do not do this detestable thing that I hate.
44.5: But they did not listen or incline their ears; they did not turn from their wickedness or stop burning incense to other gods.
44.6: Therefore My wrath and anger poured out and burned in the cities of Judah (praised) and in the streets of Jerusalem (city of peace), so that they have become the desolate ruin they are today.
44.7: So now, this is what the LORD God {YHWH Elohe} of Hosts, the God of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: Why are you? doing such great harm to yourselves by cutting off from Judah (praised) man and woman, child and infant, leaving yourselves without a remnant?
44.8: Why are you provoking Me to anger by the work of your? hands by burning incense to other gods in the land of Egypt (land of bondage), where you? have gone to reside? As a result, you will be cut off and will become an object of cursing and reproach among all the nations of the earth.
44.9: Have you? forgotten the wickedness of your? fathers and of the kings of Judah (praised) and their wives, as well as the wickedness that you? and your? wives committed in the land of Judah and in the streets of Jerusalem (city of peace)?
44.10: To this day they have not humbled themselves or shown reverence, nor have they followed My instruction or the statutes that I set before you? and your? fathers.
44.11: Therefore this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: I will set My face to bring disaster and to cut off all Judah (praised).
44.12: And I will take away the remnant of Judah (praised) who have resolved to go to the land of Egypt (land of bondage) to reside there; they will meet their end. They will all fall by the sword or be consumed by famine. From the least to the greatest, they will die by sword or famine; and they will become an object of cursing and horror, of vilification and reproach.
44.13: I will punish those who live in the land of Egypt (land of bondage), just as I punished Jerusalem (city of peace), by sword and famine and plague,
44.14: so that none of the remnant of Judah (praised) who have gone to reside in Egypt (land of bondage) will escape or survive to return to the land of Judah, where they long to return and live; for none will return except a few fugitives.”
44.15: Then all the men who knew that their wives were burning incense to other gods, and all the women standing by—a great assembly—along with all the people living in the land of Egypt (land of bondage) and in Pathros (in Lower and Upper Egypt), said to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed),
44.16: “As for the word you have spoken to us in the name of the LORD {YHWH}, we will not listen to you!
44.17: Instead, we will do everything we vowed to do: We will burn incense to the Queen of Heaven and offer drink offerings to her, just as we, our fathers, our kings, and our officials did in the cities of Judah (praised) and in the streets of Jerusalem (city of peace). At that time we had plenty of food and good things, and we saw no disaster.
44.18: But from the time we stopped burning incense to the Queen of Heaven and pouring out drink offerings to her, we have lacked everything and have been perishing by sword and famine.”
44.19: “Moreover,” said the women, “when we burned incense to the Queen of Heaven and poured out drink offerings to her, was it without our husbands’ knowledge that we made sacrificial cakes in her image and poured out drink offerings to her?
44.20: Then Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) said to all the people, both men and women, who were answering him,
44.21: “As for the incense you? burned in the cities of Judah (praised) and in the streets of Jerusalem (city of peace)—you?, your? fathers, your? kings, your? officials, and the people of the land—did the LORD {YHWH} not remember and bring this to mind?
44.22: So the LORD {YHWH} could no longer endure the evil deeds and detestable acts you? committed, and your? land became a desolation, a horror, and an object of cursing, without inhabitant, as it is this day.
44.23: Because you? burned incense and sinned against the LORD {YHWH} and did not obey the voice of the LORD or walk in His instruction, His statutes, and His testimonies, this disaster has befallen you?, as you? see today.
44.24: Then Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) said to all the people, including all the women, “Hear the word of the LORD {YHWH}, all those of Judah (praised) who are in the land of Egypt (land of bondage).
44.25: This is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: As for you? and your? wives, you? have spoken with your? mouths and fulfilled with your? hands your? words: ‘We will surely perform our vows that we have made to burn incense to the Queen of Heaven and to pour out drink offerings to her. Go ahead, then, do what you? have promised! Keep your? vows!
44.26: Nevertheless, hear the word of the LORD {YHWH}, all you people of Judah (praised) living in Egypt (land of bondage): Behold, I have sworn by My great name, says the LORD, that never again will any man of Judah living in the land of Egypt invoke My name or say, ‘As surely as the Lord GOD {Adonai YHWH} lives.
44.27: I am watching over them for harm and not for good, and every man of Judah (praised) who is in the land of Egypt (land of bondage) will meet his end by sword or famine, until they are finished off.
44.28: Those who escape the sword will return from Egypt (land of bondage) to Judah (praised), few in number, and the whole remnant of Judah who went to dwell in the land of Egypt will know whose word will stand, Mine or theirs!
44.29: This will be a sign to you? that I will punish you? in this place, declares the LORD {YHWH}, so that you? may know that My threats of harm against you? will surely stand.
44.30: This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: Behold, I will deliver Pharaoh (great house) Hophra (the great house covering evil) king of Egypt (land of bondage) into the hands of his enemies who seek his life, just as I delivered Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) king of Judah (praised) into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), the enemy who was seeking his life.

45.1: This is the word that Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet spoke to Baruch (blessed) son of Neriah (lamp of YHWH) when he wrote these words on a scroll at the dictation of Jeremiah in the fourth year of Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) son of Josiah (whom YHWH heals), king of Judah (praised):
45.2: “This is what the LORD, the God {YHWH Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says to you, Baruch (blessed):
45.3: You have said, ‘Woe is me because the LORD {YHWH} has added sorrow to my pain! I am worn out with groaning and have found no rest.
45.4: Thus Jeremiah was to say to Baruch \Thus you shall say to him:/: “This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: Throughout the land I will demolish what I have built and uproot what I have planted.
45.5: But as for you, do you seek great things for yourself? Stop seeking! For I will bring disaster on every living creature, declares the LORD {YHWH}, but wherever you go, I will grant your life as a spoil of war.

46.1: This is the word of the LORD {YHWH} about the nations—the word that came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet
46.2: concerning Egypt (land of bondage) and the army of Pharaoh (great house) Neco (lame) king of Egypt, which was defeated at Carchemish (fortress of Chemosh) on the Euphrates (fruitfulness) River by Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) in the fourth year of Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) the son of Josiah (whom YHWH heals) king of Judah (praised):
46.3: “Deploy your shields, small and large; advance for battle!
46.4: Harness the horses; mount the steeds; take your positions with helmets on! Polish your spears; put on armor!
46.5: Why am I seeing this? They are terrified, they are retreating; their warriors are defeated, they flee in haste without looking back; terror is on every side! declares the LORD {YHWH}.
46.6: “The swift cannot flee, and the warrior cannot escape! In the north by the River Euphrates (fruitfulness) they stumble and fall.
46.7: Who is this, rising like the Nile, like rivers whose waters churn?
46.8: Egypt (land of bondage) rises like the Nile, and its waters churn like rivers, boasting, ‘I will rise and cover the earth; I will destroy the cities and their people.’
46.9: Advance, O horses! Race furiously, O chariots! Let the warriors come forth—Cush (the upper Nile region) and Put (a bow) carrying their shields, men of Lydia (to the firebrands) drawing the bow.
46.10: For that day belongs to the Lord GOD {Adonai YHWH} of Hosts, a day of vengeance against His foes. The sword will devour until it is satisfied, until it is quenched with their blood. For the Lord GOD {YHWH} of Hosts will hold a sacrifice in the land of the north by the River Euphrates (fruitfulness).
46.11: Go up to Gilead (rocky region) for balm, O Virgin Daughter of Egypt (land of bondage)! In vain you try many remedies, but for you there is no healing.
46.12: The nations have heard of your shame, and your outcry fills the earth, because warrior stumbles over warrior and both of them have fallen together.
46.13: This is the word that the LORD {YHWH} spoke to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet about the coming of Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) to strike the land of Egypt (land of bondage):
46.14: “Announce it in Egypt (land of bondage), and proclaim it in Migdol (tower); proclaim it in Memphis [Noph] and Tahpanhes (You will fill hands with pity): ‘Take your positions and prepare yourself, for the sword devours those around you.
46.15: Why have your warriors been laid low? They cannot stand, for the LORD {YHWH} has thrust them down.
46.16: They continue to stumble; indeed, they have fallen over one another. They say, ‘Get up! Let us return to our people and to the land of our birth, away from the sword of the oppressor.
46.17: There they will cry out: ‘Pharaoh (great house) king of Egypt (land of bondage) was all noise; he has let the appointed time pass him by.
46.18: As surely as I live, declares the King, whose name is the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, there will come one who is like Tabor (mound) among the mountains and like Carmel (garden-land) by the sea.
46.19: Pack your bags for exile, O daughter dwelling in Egypt (land of bondage)! For Memphis (presentability) will be laid waste, destroyed and uninhabited.
46.20: Egypt (land of bondage) is a beautiful heifer, but a gadfly from the north is coming against her.
46.21: Even the mercenaries among her are like fattened calves. They too will turn back; together they will flee, they will not stand their ground, for the day of calamity is coming upon them—the time of their punishment.
46.22: Egypt will hiss like a fleeing serpent {nachash}, for the enemy will advance in force; with axes they will come against her like woodsmen cutting down trees.
46.23: They will chop down her forest, declares the LORD {YHWH}, dense though it may be, for they are more numerous than locusts; they cannot be counted.
46.24: The Daughter of Egypt (land of bondage) will be put to shame; she will be delivered into the hands of the people of the north.
46.25: The LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: “Behold, I am about to punish Amon (nourish / be faithful) god of Thebes \Amon of No/, along with Pharaoh (great house), Egypt (land of bondage) with her gods and kings, and those who trust in Pharaoh.
46.26: I will deliver them into the hands of those who seek their lives—of Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) and his officers. But after this, Egypt will be inhabited as in days of old, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
46.27: But you, O Jacob (he grasps the heel) My servant, do not be afraid, and do not be dismayed, O Israel (he wrestles with God). For I will surely save you out of a distant place, your descendants from the land of their captivity! Jacob will return to quiet and ease, with no one to make him afraid.
46.28: And you, My servant Jacob (he grasps the heel), do not be afraid, declares the LORD {YHWH}, for I am with you. Though I will completely destroy all the nations to which I have banished you, I will not completely destroy you. Yet I will discipline you justly, and will by no means leave you unpunished.

47.1: This is the word of the LORD {YHWH} that came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet about the Philistines (immigrants) before Pharaoh (great house) struck down Gaza (the strong).
47.2: This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: “See how the waters are rising from the north and becoming an overflowing torrent. They will overflow the land and its fullness, the cities and their inhabitants. The people will cry out, and all who dwell in the land will wail
47.3: at the sound of the galloping hooves of stallions, the rumbling of chariots, and the clatter of their wheels. The fathers will not turn back for their sons; their hands will hang limp.
47.4: For the day has come to destroy all the Philistines (immigrants), to cut off from Tyre (a rock) and Sidon (hunting) every remaining ally. Indeed, the LORD {YHWH} is about to destroy the Philistines, the remnant from the coasts of Caphtor (Crete).
47.5: The people of Gaza (the strong) will shave their heads in mourning; Ashkelon (the fire of infamy) will be silenced. O remnant of their valley (the Mediterranean coast), how long will you gash yourself?
47.6: ‘Alas, O sword of the LORD {YHWH}, how long until you rest? Return to your sheath; cease and be still!
47.7: How can it rest when the LORD {YHWH} has commanded it? He has appointed it against Ashkelon (the fire of infamy) and the shore of its coastland.

48.1: Concerning Moab (of his father), this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: “Woe to Nebo (prophet), for it will be devastated. Kiriathaim (two cities) will be captured and disgraced; the fortress (height) will be shattered and dismantled.
48.2: There is no longer praise for Moab (of his father); in Heshbon (Heshbon) they devise evil against her: ‘Come, let us cut her off from nationhood (nations). You too, O people of Madmen (Madmen), will be silenced; the sword will pursue you.
48.3: A voice cries out from Horonaim (two caves): ‘Devastation and great destruction!
48.4: Moab (of his father) will be shattered; her little ones will cry out [her cries are heard as far away as Zoar].
48.5: For on the ascent to Luhith (tablets) they weep bitterly as they go, and on the descent to Horonaim (two caves) cries of distress resound over the destruction:
48.6: ‘Flee! Run for your? lives! Become like a juniper in the desert (like a wild donkey).
48.7: Because you trust in your works and treasures, you too will be captured, and Chemosh (subduer) will go into exile with his priests and officials.
48.8: The destroyer will move against every city, and not one town will escape. The valley will also be ruined, and the high plain will be destroyed, as the LORD {YHWH} has said.
48.9: Put salt on Moab (of his father), for she will be laid waste (Give wings to Moab, for she would fly away); her cities will become desolate, with no one to dwell in them.
48.10: Cursed is the one who is remiss in doing the work of the LORD {YHWH}, and cursed is he who withholds his sword from bloodshed.
48.11: Moab (of his father) has been at ease from youth, settled like wine on its dregs; he has not been poured from vessel to vessel or gone into exile. So his flavor has remained the same, and his aroma is unchanged.
48.12: Therefore behold, the days are coming, declares the LORD {YHWH}, when I will send to him wanderers, who will pour him out. They will empty his vessels and shatter his jars.
48.13: Then Moab (of his father) will be ashamed of Chemosh (subduer), just as the house of Israel (he wrestles with God) (house of God) was ashamed when they trusted in Bethel.
48.14: How can you? say, ‘We are warriors, mighty men ready for battle’
48.15: Moab (of his father) has been destroyed and its towns have been invaded; the best of its young men have gone down in the slaughter, declares the King, whose name is the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts.
48.16: Moab’s (of his father) calamity is at hand, and his affliction is rushing swiftly.
48.17: Mourn for him, all you who surround him, everyone who knows his name; tell how the mighty scepter is shattered—the glorious staff!
48.18: Come down from your glory; sit on parched ground, O daughter dwelling in Dibon (wasting), for the destroyer of Moab (of his father) has come against you; he has destroyed your fortresses.
48.19: Stand by the road and watch, O dweller of Aroer (ruins)! Ask the man fleeing or the woman escaping, ‘What has happened?
48.20: Moab (of his father) is put to shame, for it has been shattered. Wail and cry out! Declare by the Arnon (rushing stream) that Moab is destroyed.
48.21: Judgment has come upon the high plain—upon Holon (sandy), Jahzah (Jahaz), and Mephaath (splendor / height),
48.22: upon Dibon (wasting), Nebo (prophet), and Beth-diblathaim (house of the two fig-cakes),
48.23: upon Kiriathaim (two cities), Beth-gamul (house of recompense), and Beth-meon (house of Baal),
48.24: upon Kerioth (cities), Bozrah (sheepfold / fortress), and all the towns of Moab (of his father), those far and near.
48.25: The horn of Moab (of his father) has been cut off, and his arm is broken, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
48.26: “Make him drunk, because he has magnified himself against the LORD {YHWH}; so Moab (of his father) will wallow in his own vomit, and he will also become a laughingstock.
48.27: Was not Israel (he wrestles with God) your object of ridicule? Was he ever found among thieves? For whenever you speak of him you shake your head.
48.28: Abandon the towns and settle among the rocks, O dwellers of Moab (of his father)! Be like a dove that nests at the mouth of a cave.
48.29: We have heard of Moab’s (of his father) pomposity, his exceeding pride and conceit, his proud arrogance and haughtiness of heart.
48.30: I know his insolence, declares the LORD {YHWH}, “but it is futile. His boasting is as empty as his deeds.
48.31: Therefore I will wail for Moab (of his father); I will cry out for all of Moab; I will moan for the men of Kir-heres (wall of potsherds).
48.32: I will weep for you, O vine of Sibmah (fragrance), more than I weep for Jazer (helped). Your tendrils have extended to the sea (Dead Sea); they reach even to Jazer [to the Sea of Jazer]. The destroyer has descended on your summer fruit and grape harvest.
48.33: Joy and gladness are removed from the orchard and from the fields of Moab (of his father). I have stopped the flow of wine from the presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy; their shouts are not for joy.
48.34: There is a cry from Heshbon (stronghold) to Elealeh (God is ascending); they raise their voices to Jahaz (trodden down), from Zoar (insignificance) to Horonaim (two caves) and Eglath-shelishiyah; for even the waters of Nimrim (pure) have dried up.
48.35: In Moab (of his father), declares the LORD {YHWH}, I will bring an end to those who make offerings on the high places and burn incense to their gods.
48.36: Therefore My heart laments like a flute for Moab (of his father); it laments like a flute for the men of Kir-heres (wall of potsherds) because the wealth they acquired has perished.
48.37: For every head is shaved and every beard is clipped; on every hand is a gash, and around every waist is sackcloth.
48.38: On all the rooftops of Moab (of his father) and in the public squares, everyone is mourning; for I have shattered Moab like an unwanted jar, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
48.39: “How shattered it is! How they wail! How Moab (of his father) has turned his back in shame! Moab has become an object of ridicule and horror to all those around him.”
48.40: For this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: “Behold, an eagle swoops down and spreads his wings against Moab (of his father).
48.41: Kirioth (cities) has been taken, and the strongholds seized. In that day the heart of Moab’s (of his father) warriors will be like the heart of a woman in labor.
48.42: Moab (of his father) will be destroyed as a nation because he vaunted himself against the LORD {YHWH}.
48.43: Terror and pit and snare await you, O dweller of Moab (of his father), declares the LORD {YHWH}.
48.44: “Whoever flees the panic will fall into the pit, and whoever climbs from the pit will be caught in the snare. For I will bring upon Moab (of his father) the year of their punishment, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
48.45: “Those who flee will stand helpless in Heshbon’s (stronghold) shadow, because fire has gone forth from Heshbon and a flame from within Sihon (warrior). It devours the foreheads of Moab (of his father) and the skulls of the sons of tumult.
48.46: Woe to you, O Moab (of his father)! The people of Chemosh (subduer) have perished; for your sons have been taken into exile and your daughters have gone into captivity.
48.47: Yet in the latter days I will restore Moab (of his father) from captivity (restore the fortunes of Moab), declares the LORD {YHWH}. Here ends the judgment on Moab.

49.1: Concerning the Ammonites (tribal), this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: “Has Israel (he wrestles with God) no sons? Is he without heir? Why then has Milcom (their king) taken possession of Gad (troop)? Why have his people settled in their cities?
49.2: Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares the LORD {YHWH}, when I will sound the battle cry against Rabbah (great) of the Ammonites (tribal). It will become a heap of ruins, and its villages will be burned. Then Israel (he wrestles with God) will drive out their dispossessors, says the LORD.
49.3: Wail, O Heshbon (stronghold), for Ai (heap of ruins) has been destroyed; cry out, O daughters of Rabbah (great)! Put on sackcloth and mourn; run back and forth within your walls, for Milcom will go into exile together with his priests and officials.
49.4: Why do you boast of your valleys—your valleys so fruitful (your valleys flowing), O faithless daughter? You trust in your riches and say, ‘Who can come against me?
49.5: Behold, I am about to bring terror upon you, declares the Lord GOD {Adonai YHWH} of Hosts, from all those around you. You? will each be driven headlong, with no one to regather the fugitives.
49.6: Yet afterward I will restore the Ammonites (tribal) from captivity (restore the fortunes of the Ammonites), declares the LORD {YHWH}.
49.7: Concerning Edom (red), this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: “Is there no longer wisdom in Teman (south)? Has counsel perished from the prudent? Has their wisdom decayed?
49.8: Turn and run! Lie low, O dwellers of Dedan (low country), for I will bring disaster on Esau (hairy) at the time I punish him.
49.9: If grape gatherers came to you, would they not leave some gleanings? Were thieves to come in the night, would they not steal only what they wanted?
49.10: But I will strip Esau (hairy) bare; I will uncover his hiding places, and he will be unable to conceal himself. His descendants will be destroyed along with his relatives and neighbors, and he will be no more.
49.11: Abandon your orphans; I will preserve their lives. Let your widows trust in Me.
49.12: For this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: “If those who do not deserve to drink the cup must drink it, can you possibly remain unpunished? You will not go unpunished, for you must drink it too.
49.13: For by Myself I have sworn, declares the LORD {YHWH}, that Bozrah (sheepfold / fortress) will become a desolation, a disgrace, a ruin, and a curse, and all her cities will be in ruins forever.”
49.14: I have heard a message from the LORD {YHWH}; an envoy has been sent to the nations: “Assemble yourselves to march against her! Rise up for battle!
49.15: “For behold, I will make you small among nations, despised among men.
49.16: The terror you cause and the pride of your heart have deceived you, O dwellers in the clefts of the rocks (of Sela), O occupiers of the mountain summit. Though you elevate your nest like the eagle, even from there I will bring you down, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
49.17: “Edom (red) will become an object of horror. All who pass by will be appalled and will scoff at all her wounds.
49.18: As Sodom (burning) and Gomorrah (submersion) were overthrown along with their neighbors,” says the LORD {YHWH}, “no one will dwell there; no man will abide there.
49.19: Behold, one will come up like a lion from the thickets of the Jordan (flowing down) to the watered pasture. For in an instant I will chase Edom from her land. Who is the chosen one I will appoint for this? For who is like Me, and who can challenge Me? What shepherd can stand against Me?”
49.20: Therefore hear the plans that the LORD {YHWH} has drawn up against Edom (red) and the strategies He has devised against the people of Teman (south): Surely the little ones of the flock will be dragged away; certainly their pasture will be made desolate because of them (their pasture will be appalled at their fate).
49.21: At the sound of their fall the earth will quake; their cry will resound to the Red Sea (the Sea of Reeds).
49.22: Look! An eagle will soar and swoop down, spreading its wings over Bozrah (sheepfold / fortress). In that day the hearts of Edom’s (red) mighty men will be like the heart of a woman in labor.
49.23: Concerning Damascus (well-watered land): “Hamath (fortress) and Arpad (I shall be supported) are put to shame, for they have heard a bad report; they are agitated like the sea \on the sea/; their anxiety cannot be calmed.
49.24: Damascus (well-watered land) has become feeble; she has turned to flee. Panic has gripped her; anguish and pain have seized her like a woman in labor.
49.25: How is the city of praise not forsaken, the town that brings Me joy?
49.26: For her young men will fall in the streets, and all her warriors will be silenced in that day, declares the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts.
49.27: “I will set fire to the walls of Damascus (well-watered land); it will consume the fortresses of Ben-hadad (son of Hadad).
49.28: Concerning Kedar (dark) and the kingdoms of Hazor (castle), which Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) defeated, this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: “Rise up, advance against Kedar, and destroy the people of the east!
49.29: They will take their tents and flocks, their tent curtains and all their goods. They will take their camels for themselves. They will shout to them: ‘Terror is on every side!
49.30: Run! Escape quickly! Lie low, O residents of Hazor (castle), declares the LORD {YHWH}, “for Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) has drawn up a plan against you?; he has devised a strategy against you?.
49.31: Rise up, advance against a nation at ease, one that dwells securely, declares the LORD {YHWH}. “They have no gates or bars; they live alone.
49.32: Their camels will become plunder, and their large herds will be spoil. I will scatter to the wind {ruach} in every direction those who shave their temples; I will bring calamity on them from all sides, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
49.33: “Hazor (castle) will become a haunt for jackals (serpents), a desolation forever. No one will dwell there; no man will abide there.
49.34: This is the word of the LORD {YHWH} that came to Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet concerning Elam (eternity) at the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) king of Judah (praised).
49.35: This is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: “Behold, I will shatter Elam’s (eternity) bow, the mainstay of their might.
49.36: I will bring the four winds {ruachowt} against Elam (eternity) from the four corners of the heavens, and I will scatter them to all these winds. There will not be a nation to which Elam’s exiles will not go.
49.37: So I will shatter Elam (eternity) before their foes, before those who seek their lives. I will bring disaster upon them, even My fierce anger, declares the LORD {YHWH}. “I will send out the sword after them until I finish them off.
49.38: I will set My throne in Elam (eternity), and destroy its king and officials, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
49.39: “Yet in the last days, I will restore Elam (eternity) from captivity (restore the fortunes of Elam), declares the LORD {YHWH}.

50.1: This is the word that the LORD {YHWH} spoke through Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet concerning Babylon (confusion by mixing) and the land of the Chaldeans (the Babylonians):
50.2: “Announce and declare to the nations; lift up a banner and proclaim it; hold nothing back when you say, ‘Babylon (confusion by mixing) is captured; Bel (lord) is put to shame; Marduk (your rebellion) is shattered, her images are disgraced, her idols are broken in pieces.
50.3: For a nation from the north will come against her; it will make her land a desolation. No one will live in it; both man and beast will flee.
50.4: “In those days and at that time, declares the LORD {YHWH}, the children of Israel (he wrestles with God) and the children of Judah (praised) will come together, weeping as they come, and will seek the LORD their God {YHWH Elohehem}.
50.5: They will ask the way to Zion and turn their faces toward it. They will come and join themselves to the LORD {YHWH} in an everlasting covenant that will never be forgotten.
50.6: My people are lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray, causing them to roam the mountains. They have wandered from mountain to hill; they have forgotten their resting place.
50.7: All who found them devoured them, and their enemies said, ‘We are not guilty, for they have sinned against the LORD {YHWH}, their true pasture, the LORD, the hope of their fathers.
50.8: Flee from the midst of Babylon (confusion by mixing); depart from the land of the Chaldeans (clod-breakers); be like the he-goats that lead the flock.
50.9: For behold, I stir up and bring against Babylon (confusion by mixing) an assembly of great nations from the land of the north. They will line up against her; from the north she will be captured. Their arrows will be like skilled warriors who do not return empty-handed.
50.10: Chaldea (Babylonia) will be plundered; all who plunder her will have their fill, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
50.11: “Because you? rejoice, because you? sing in triumph—you who plunder My inheritance—because you? frolic like a heifer treading grain and neigh like stallions,
50.12: your? mother will be greatly ashamed; she who bore you? will be disgraced. Behold, she will be the least of the nations, a wilderness, a dry land, and a desert.
50.13: Because of the wrath of the LORD {YHWH}, she will not be inhabited; she will become completely desolate. All who pass through Babylon (confusion by mixing) will be horrified and will hiss at all her wounds.
50.14: Line up in formation around Babylon (confusion by mixing), all you who draw the bow! Shoot at her! Spare no arrows! For she has sinned against the LORD {YHWH}.
50.15: Raise a war cry against her on every side! She has thrown up her hands in surrender; her towers have fallen; her walls are torn down. Since this is the vengeance of the LORD {YHWH}, take out your vengeance upon her; as she has done, do the same to her.
50.16: Cut off the sower from Babylon (confusion by mixing), and the one who wields the sickle at harvest time. In the face of the oppressor’s sword, each will turn to his own people, each will flee to his own land.
50.17: Israel (he wrestles with God) is a scattered flock, chased away by lions. The first to devour him was the king of Assyria (a step); the last to crush his bones was Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing).
50.18: Therefore this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: “I will punish the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) and his land as I punished the king of Assyria (a step).
50.19: I will return Israel (he wrestles with God) to his pasture, and he will graze on Carmel (garden-land) and Bashan (fruitful); his soul will be satisfied on the hills of Ephraim (doubly fruitful) and Gilead (rocky region).
50.20: In those days and at that time, declares the LORD {YHWH}, a search will be made for Israel’s (he wrestles with God) guilt, but there will be none, and for Judah’s (praised) sins, but they will not be found; for I will forgive the remnant I preserve.
50.21: Go up against the land of Merathaim (double rebellion), and against the residents of Pekod (visitation). Kill them [Avenge, O sword,] and devote them to destruction {cherem}. Do all that I have commanded you, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
50.22: “The noise of battle is in the land—the noise of great destruction.
50.23: How the hammer of the whole earth lies broken and shattered! What a horror Babylon (confusion by mixing) has become among the nations!
50.24: I laid a snare for you, O Babylon (confusion by mixing), and you were caught before you knew it. You were found and captured because you challenged the LORD {YHWH}.
50.25: The LORD {YHWH} has opened His armory and brought out His weapons of wrath, for this is the work of the Lord GOD {Adonai YHWH} of Hosts in the land of the Chaldeans (clod-breakers).
50.26: Come against her from the farthest border. Break open her granaries; pile her up like mounds of grain. Devote her to destruction; leave her no survivors.
50.27: Kill all her young bulls; let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them, for their day has come—the time of their punishment.
50.28: Listen to the fugitives and refugees from the land of Babylon (confusion by mixing), declaring in Zion the vengeance of the LORD our God {YHWH Elohenu}, the vengeance for His temple.
50.29: Summon the archers against Babylon (confusion by mixing), all who string the bow. Encamp all around her; let no one escape. Repay her according to her deeds; do to her as she has done. For she has defied the LORD {YHWH}, the Holy One of Israel (he wrestles with God).
50.30: Therefore, her young men will fall in the streets, and all her warriors will be silenced in that day, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
50.31: “Behold, I am against you, O arrogant one, declares the Lord GOD {Adonai YHWH} of Hosts, “for your day has come, the time when I will punish you.
50.32: The arrogant one will stumble and fall with no one to pick him up. And I will kindle a fire in his cities to consume all those around him.”
50.33: This is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: “The sons of Israel (he wrestles with God) are oppressed, and the sons of Judah (praised) as well. All their captors hold them fast, refusing to release them.
50.34: Their Redeemer is strong; the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts is His name. He will fervently plead their case so that He may bring rest to the earth, but turmoil to those who live in Babylon (confusion by mixing).
50.35: A sword is against the Chaldeans (clod-breakers), declares the LORD {YHWH}, against those who live in Babylon (confusion by mixing), and against her officials and wise men.
50.36: A sword is against her false prophets, and they will become fools. A sword is against her warriors, and they will be filled with terror.
50.37: A sword is against her horses and chariots and against all the foreigners in her midst, and they will become like women. A sword is against her treasuries, and they will be plundered.
50.38: A drought is upon her waters, and they will be dried up. For it is a land of graven images, and the people go mad over idols (go mad with fear).
50.39: So the desert creatures and hyenas will live there and ostriches \daughters of an ostrich/ will dwell there. It will never again be inhabited or lived in from generation to generation.
50.40: As God {Elohim} overthrew Sodom (burning) and Gomorrah (submersion) along with their neighbors, declares the LORD {YHWH}, “no one will dwell there; no man will abide there.
50.41: Behold, an army is coming from the north; a great nation and many kings are stirred up from the ends of the earth.
50.42: They grasp the bow and spear (javelin); they are cruel and merciless. Their voice roars like the sea, and they ride upon horses, lined up like men in formation against you, O Daughter of Babylon (confusion by mixing).
50.43: The king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) has heard the report, and his hands hang limp. Anguish has gripped him, pain like that of a woman in labor.
50.44: Behold, one will come up like a lion from the thickets of the Jordan (flowing down) to the watered pasture. For in an instant I will chase Babylon from her land. Who is the chosen one I will appoint for this? For who is like Me, and who can challenge Me? What shepherd can stand against Me?”
50.45: Therefore hear the plans that the LORD {YHWH} has drawn up against Babylon (confusion by mixing) and the strategies He has devised against the land of the Chaldeans (clod-breakers): Surely the little ones of the flock will be dragged away; certainly their pasture will be made desolate because of them.
50.46: At the sound of Babylon’s (confusion by mixing) capture the earth will quake; a cry will be heard among the nations.

51.1: This is what the LORD {YHWH} says: “Behold, I will stir up against Babylon (confusion by mixing) and against the people of Leb-kamai (Leb-kamai) the spirit {ruach} of a destroyer.
51.2: I will send strangers to Babylon (confusion by mixing) to winnow her and empty her land; for they will come against her from every side in her day of disaster.
51.3: Do not let the archer bend his bow or put on his armor. Do not spare her young men; devote all her army to destruction {cherem}!
51.4: And they will fall slain in the land of the Chaldeans (the Babylonians), and pierced through in her streets.
51.5: For Israel (he wrestles with God) and Judah (praised) have not been abandoned by their God, the LORD {Elohim YHWH} of Hosts, though their land is full of guilt before the Holy One of Israel.
51.6: Flee from Babylon (confusion by mixing)! Escape with your lives! Do not be destroyed in her punishment. For this is the time of the LORD’s {YHWH} vengeance; He will pay her what she deserves.
51.7: Babylon (confusion by mixing) was a gold cup in the hand of the LORD {YHWH}, making the whole earth drunk. The nations drank her wine; therefore the nations have gone mad.
51.8: Suddenly Babylon (confusion by mixing) has fallen and been shattered. Wail for her; get her balm for her pain; perhaps she can be healed.
51.9: “We tried to heal Babylon (confusion by mixing), but she could not be healed. Abandon her! Let each of us go to his own land, for her judgment extends to the sky and reaches to the clouds.
51.10: “The LORD {YHWH} has brought forth our vindication; come, let us tell in Zion what the LORD our God {Elohenu} has accomplished.
51.11: Sharpen the arrows! Fill the quivers [Fill the hand with the shields!]! The LORD {YHWH} has aroused the spirit {ruach} of the kings of the Medes (middle land), because His plan is aimed at Babylon (confusion by mixing) to destroy her, for it is the vengeance of the LORD—vengeance for His temple.
51.12: Raise a banner against the walls of Babylon (confusion by mixing); post the guard; station the watchmen; prepare the ambush. For the LORD {YHWH} has both devised and accomplished what He spoke against the people of Babylon.
51.13: You who dwell by many waters, rich in treasures, your end has come; the thread of your life is cut.
51.14: The LORD {YHWH} of Hosts has sworn by Himself: “Surely I will fill you up with men as with locusts, and they will shout in triumph over you.
51.15: The LORD made the earth by His power; He established the world by His wisdom and stretched out the heavens by His understanding.
51.16: When He thunders, the waters in the heavens roar; He causes the clouds to rise from the ends of the earth. He generates the lightning with the rain and brings forth the wind {ruach} from His storehouses.
51.17: Every man is senseless and devoid of knowledge; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols. For his molten images are a fraud, and there is no breath {ruach} in them.
51.18: They are worthless, a work to be mocked. In the time of their punishment they will perish.
51.19: The Portion of Jacob (he grasps the heel) is not like these, for He is the Maker of all things, and of the tribe of His inheritance—the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts is His name.
51.20: “You are My war club, My weapon for battle. With you I shatter nations; with you I bring kingdoms to ruin.
51.21: With you I shatter the horse and rider; with you I shatter the chariot and driver.
51.22: With you I shatter man and woman; with you I shatter the old man and the youth; with you I shatter the young man and the maiden.
51.23: With you I shatter the shepherd and his flock; with you I shatter the farmer and his oxen; with you I shatter the governors and officials.
51.24: Before your? very eyes I will repay Babylon (confusion by mixing) and all the dwellers of Chaldea (Babylonia) for all the evil they have done in Zion,” declares the LORD {YHWH}.
51.25: “Behold, I am against you, O destroying mountain, you who devastate the whole earth, declares the LORD {YHWH}. I will stretch out My hand against you; I will roll you over the cliffs and turn you into a charred mountain.
51.26: No one shall retrieve from you a cornerstone or a foundation stone, because you will become desolate forever, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
51.27: “Raise a banner in the land! Blow the ram’s horn among the nations! Prepare the nations against her. Summon the kingdoms against her—Ararat (curse reversed), Minni (division), and Ashkenaz. Appoint a captain against her; bring up horses like swarming locusts.
51.28: Prepare the nations for battle against her—the kings of the Medes (middle land), their governors and all their officials, and all the lands they rule.
51.29: The earth quakes and writhes because the LORD’s {YHWH} intentions against Babylon (confusion by mixing) stand: to make the land of Babylon a desolation, without inhabitant.
51.30: The warriors of Babylon (confusion by mixing) have stopped fighting; they sit in their strongholds. Their strength is exhausted; they have become like women. Babylon’s homes have been set ablaze, the bars of her gates are broken.
