51.31: One courier races to meet another, and messenger follows messenger, to announce to the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) that his city has been captured from end to end.
51.32: The fords have been seized, the marshes set on fire, and the soldiers are terrified.
51.33: For this is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts, the God {Elohe} of Israel (he wrestles with God), says: “The Daughter of Babylon (confusion by mixing) is like a threshing floor at the time it is trampled. In just a little while her harvest time will come.
51.34: “Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) has devoured me; he has crushed me. He has set me aside like an empty vessel; he has swallowed me like a monster; he filled his belly with my delicacies and vomited me out (expelled me).
51.35: May the violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon (confusion by mixing),” says the dweller of Zion. “May my blood be on the dwellers of Chaldea (clod-breakers),” says Jerusalem (city of peace).
51.36: Therefore this is what the LORD {YHWH} says: “Behold, I will plead your case and take vengeance on your behalf; I will dry up her sea and make her springs run dry.
51.37: Babylon (confusion by mixing) will become a heap of rubble, a haunt for jackals (serpents), an object of horror and scorn, without inhabitant.
51.38: They will roar together like young lions; they will growl like lion cubs.
51.39: While they are flushed with heat, I will serve them a feast, and I will make them drunk so that they may revel; then they will fall asleep forever and never wake up, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
51.40: I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter, like rams with male goats.
51.41: How Sheshach (Sheshach) has been captured! The praise of all the earth has been seized. What a horror Babylon (confusion by mixing) has become among the nations!
51.42: The sea has come up over Babylon (confusion by mixing); she is covered in turbulent waves.
51.43: Her cities have become a desolation, a dry and arid land, a land where no one lives, where no son of man passes through.
51.44: I will punish Bel (lord) in Babylon (confusion by mixing). I will make him spew out what he swallowed. The nations will no longer stream to him; even the wall of Babylon will fall.
51.45: Come out of her, My people ! Save your lives, each of you, from the fierce anger of the LORD {YHWH}.
51.46: Do not let your? heart grow faint, and do not be afraid when the rumor is heard in the land; for a rumor will come one year—and then another the next year—of violence in the land and of ruler against ruler.
51.47: Therefore, behold, the days are coming when I will punish the idols of Babylon (confusion by mixing). Her entire land will suffer shame, and all her slain will lie fallen within her.
51.48: Then heaven and earth and all that is in them will shout for joy over Babylon (confusion by mixing) because the destroyers from the north will come against her, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
51.49: “Babylon (confusion by mixing) must fall on account of the slain of Israel (he wrestles with God), just as the slain of all the earth have fallen because of Babylon.
51.50: You? who have escaped the sword, depart and do not linger! Remember the LORD {YHWH} from far away, and let Jerusalem (city of peace) come to mind.
51.51: “We are ashamed because we have heard reproach; disgrace has covered our faces, because foreigners have entered the holy places of the LORD’s {YHWH} house.
51.52: “Therefore, behold, the days are coming, declares the LORD {YHWH}, “when I will punish her idols, and throughout her land the wounded will groan.
51.53: Even if Babylon (confusion by mixing) ascends to the heavens and fortifies her lofty stronghold, the destroyers I send will come against her, declares the LORD {YHWH}.
51.54: “The sound of a cry comes from Babylon (confusion by mixing), the sound of great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans (clod-breakers)!
51.55: For the LORD {YHWH} will destroy Babylon (confusion by mixing); He will silence her mighty voice. The waves will roar like great waters; the tumult of their voices will resound.
51.56: For a destroyer is coming against her—against Babylon (confusion by mixing). Her warriors will be captured, and their bows will be broken, for the LORD {YHWH} is a God of retribution; He will repay in full.
51.57: I will make her princes and wise men drunk, along with her governors, officials, and warriors. Then they will fall asleep forever and not wake up, declares the King, whose name is the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts.
51.58: This is what the LORD {YHWH} of Hosts says: “Babylon’s (confusion by mixing) thick walls will be leveled, and her high gates consumed by fire. So the labor of the people will be for nothing; the nations will exhaust themselves to fuel the flames.
51.59: This is the message that Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) the prophet gave (YHWH is ruler) to the quartermaster Seraiah son of Neriah (lamp of YHWH), the son of Mahseiah (YHWH is a shelter), when he went to Babylon (confusion by mixing) with King Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) of Judah (praised) in the fourth year of Zedekiah’s reign.
51.60: Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) had written on a single scroll about all the disaster that would come upon Babylon (confusion by mixing)—all these words that had been written concerning Babylon.
51.61: And Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed) said to Seraiah (YHWH is ruler), “When you get to Babylon (confusion by mixing), see that you read all these words aloud,
51.62: and say, ‘O LORD {YHWH}, You have promised to cut off this place so that no one will remain—neither man nor beast. Indeed, it will be desolate forever.’
51.63: When you finish reading this scroll, tie a stone to it and cast it into the Euphrates (fruitfulness).
51.64: Then you are to say, ‘In the same way Babylon (confusion by mixing) will sink and never rise again, because of the disaster I will bring upon her. And her people will grow weary. Here end the words of Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed).

52.1: Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) was twenty-one years old when he became king, and he reigned in Jerusalem (city of peace) eleven years. His mother’s name was Hamutal (father-in-law is protection) daughter of Jeremiah (whom YHWH has appointed); she was from Libnah (pavement).
52.2: And Zedekiah did evil in the sight of the LORD {YHWH}, just as Jehoiakim (YHWH raises up) had done.
52.3: For because of the anger of the LORD {YHWH}, all this happened in Jerusalem (city of peace) and Judah (praised), until He finally banished them from His presence. And Zedekiah also (YHWH is righteous) rebelled against the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing).
52.4: So in the ninth year of Zedekiah’s reign, on the tenth day of the tenth month, Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) marched against Jerusalem (city of peace) with his entire army. They encamped outside the city and built a siege wall all around it.
52.5: And the city was kept under siege until King Zedekiah’s (YHWH is righteous) eleventh year.
52.6: By the ninth day of the fourth month, the famine in the city was so severe that the people of the land had no food.
52.7: Then the city was breached; and though the Chaldeans (the Babylonians) had surrounded the city, all the men of war fled the city by night by way of the gate between the two walls near the king’s garden. They headed toward the Arabah (the Jordan Valley),
52.8: but the army of the Chaldeans (clod-breakers) pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) in the plains of Jericho (city of palms), and his whole army deserted him.
52.9: The Chaldeans seized the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) at Riblah (fertility) in the land of Hamath (fortress), where he pronounced judgment on Zedekiah.
52.10: There at Riblah (fertility) the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah (YHWH is righteous) before his eyes, and he also killed all the officials of Judah (praised).
52.11: Then he put out Zedekiah’s (YHWH is righteous) eyes, bound him with bronze shackles, and took him to Babylon (confusion by mixing), where he kept him in custody until his dying day.
52.12: On the tenth day of the fifth month, in the nineteenth year of Nebuchadnezzar’s (may Nebo protect the crown) reign over Babylon (confusion by mixing), Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard, a servant of the king of Babylon, entered Jerusalem (city of peace).
52.13: He burned down the house of the LORD {YHWH}, the royal palace, and all the houses of Jerusalem (city of peace)—every significant building.
52.14: And the whole army of the Chaldeans (clod-breakers) under the captain of the guard broke down all the walls around Jerusalem (city of peace).
52.15: Then Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard carried into exile some of the poorest people and those who remained in the city, along with the deserters who had defected to the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) and the rest of the craftsmen.
52.16: But Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard left behind some of the poorest of the land to tend the vineyards and fields.
52.17: Moreover, the Chaldeans (clod-breakers) broke up the bronze pillars and {YHWH} stands and the bronze Sea in the house of the LORD, and they carried all the bronze to Babylon (confusion by mixing).
52.18: They also took away the pots, shovels, wick trimmers, sprinkling bowls, dishes, and all the articles of bronze used in the temple service.
52.19: The captain of the guard also took away the basins, censers, sprinkling bowls, pots, lampstands, pans, and drink offering bowls—anything made of pure gold or fine silver.
52.20: As for the two pillars, the Sea, the twelve bronze bulls under it, and the movable stands that King Solomon (peace) had made for the house of the LORD {YHWH}, the weight of the bronze from all these articles was beyond measure.
52.21: Each pillar was eighteen cubits tall and twelve cubits in circumference <27 ft high x 18 ft around / 8.2 m x 5.5 m around>; each was hollow, four fingers thick <2.9 in / 7.4 cm>.
52.22: The bronze capital atop one pillar was five cubits high <7.5 ft / 2.3 m>, with a network of bronze pomegranates all around. The second pillar, with its pomegranates, was similar.
52.23: Each capital had ninety-six pomegranates on the sides {ruachah}, and a total of a hundred pomegranates were above the surrounding network.
52.24: The captain of the guard also took away Seraiah (YHWH is ruler) the chief priest, Zephaniah (YHWH has treasured) the priest of second rank, and the three doorkeepers.
52.25: Of those still in the city, he took a court official who had been appointed over the men of war, as well as seven trusted royal advisers. He also took the scribe of the captain of the army, who had enlisted the people of the land, and sixty men who were found in the city.
52.26: Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) at Riblah (fertility).
52.27: There at Riblah (fertility) in the land of Hamath (fortress), the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) struck them down and put them to death. So Judah (praised) was taken into exile, away from its own land.
52.28: These are the people Nebuchadnezzar (may Nebo protect the crown) carried away: in the seventh year, 3,023 Jews;
52.29: in Nebuchadnezzar’s (may Nebo protect the crown) eighteenth year, 832 people from Jerusalem (city of peace);
52.30: in Nebuchadnezzar’s (may Nebo protect the crown) twenty-third year, Nebuzaradan (Nebo has given seed) captain of the guard carried away 745 Jews. So in all, 4,600 people were taken away.
52.31: On the twenty-fifth day of the twelfth month of the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin (YHWH establishes) king of Judah (praised), in the first year of the reign of Evil-merodach (man of Merodach) king of Babylon (confusion by mixing), he pardoned \he lifted up the head of/ Jehoiachin king of Judah and released him from prison.
52.32: And he spoke kindly to Jehoiachin and set his throne above the thrones of the other kings who were with him in Babylon (confusion by mixing).
52.33: So Jehoiachin changed out of his prison clothes, and he dined regularly at the king’s table for the rest of his life.
52.34: And the king of Babylon (confusion by mixing) provided Jehoiachin a daily portion for the rest of his life, until the day of his death.
